items.title: A small, fast-to-review prompt PR
items.scenario: You tighten the system prompt to reduce hallucinations.
items.explanation: Title: 'Tighten QA system prompt to reduce hallucinations'. Diff: 8 lines. Description: 'Reduced hallucinations on holdout (3.2% → 1.8%); eval log attached.' Reviewer can sign off in 2 minutes.

items.title: A model-version bump PR
items.explanation: 1-line code change + 30-line eval delta in the description. Reviewer checks the eval table, confirms no regressions, approves. Without the eval, this would be a leap of faith.

items.title: Using draft PRs for early feedback
items.scenario: You're partway through a refactor and want directional input before going further.
items.explanation: Open as **Draft** — signals 'don't review line-by-line yet'. Ask one teammate for high-level feedback. Mark ready for review when the diff settles.

items.title: Suggestion blocks
items.scenario: Reviewer spots three typos and a tiny rename.
items.explanation: Use GitHub's `\`\`\`suggestion` blocks. The author clicks 'Commit suggestion' four times — faster than a round-trip of comments.

items.title: Branch-protection saving the day
items.scenario: An exhausted engineer tries to push directly to `main` at midnight.
items.explanation: Branch protection rejects the push: 'protected branch hook declined'. They open a PR instead; CI catches a test regression. Process saved them from a 2am rollback.
